data.title: The Great Goat Caper
data.premise: You, Captain Mal Reynolds, and your crew take a simple job to steal a prized goat from a pompous noble on Persephone. But when the goat turns out to be a genetically engineered 'super-goat' with chaotic abilities, the heist spirals into a series of hilarious mishaps.
data.central_question: Can you and your crew successfully steal the goat, get paid, and escape without the whole affair turning into a complete goat-astrophe?
data.ending_shape: heist resolved with a comedic twist

setting.name: The Verse, Persephone
setting.description: Persephone is a bustling border planet where high society rubs elbows with smugglers and scoundrels. The story moves from the opulent estates of the noble district to the dusty landing pads where Serenity is docked.
setting.tone_notes: Lighthearted and comedic, full of witty banter, slapstick humor, and the crew's endearing dysfunction. The mood is playful, with chaos that's more funny than dangerous.

acts.title: A Baa-d Idea
acts.description: You're hired by the eccentric Lord Pompfrey to steal a rival's prized goat for a foolish wager. The crew debates the job over a chaotic dinner party, leading to a half-baked plan.
acts.end_condition: The crew agrees to the heist and sets out for the rival estate.
locked_constraints: The goat is not yet stolen
The Alliance is not directly involved
key_beats: Meet Lord Pompfrey and learn of the bet
Crew discussion reveals doubts and humor
Inara uses her skills to gather intel
Departure for the heist with reluctant enthusiasm

acts.title: Goat Gone Wild
acts.description: The crew infiltrates the estate, but the goat is unexpectedly agile and clever, leading to a series of comedic chases and near-misses with security.
acts.end_condition: The goat is captured, but the escape route is blocked by alerted guards.
locked_constraints: The goat's full abilities are not yet revealed
The crew has not escaped the estate
key_beats: Sneak in with Kaylee disabling gadgets
Jayne's arrogance leads to goat outsmarting him
River senses the goat's unusual intelligence
Cornered with the goat in a greenhouse

acts.title: Escape from Goat-astrophe
acts.description: Lord Pompfrey double-crosses you, refusing payment. With guards closing in, you must use the goat's chaotic abilities and crew ingenuity to flee and possibly turn the tables.
acts.end_condition: The crew escapes Persephone on Serenity, with or without payment, leaving behind a mess.
locked_constraints: The conflict is not fully resolved until escape
key_beats: Goat's genetic traits cause hilarious chaos
Zoe and Jayne fight a comedic retreat
Escape with the goat or leave it, learning a silly lesson

npcs.name: Zoe Washburne
npcs.motivation: Loyalty to Mal and keeping the crew safe, though she finds the job ridiculous.
npcs.secret: She's secretly betting with Wash on how long the heist will take.

npcs.name: Hoban 'Wash' Washburne
npcs.motivation: Have fun and support Zoe, while enjoying the absurdity.
npcs.secret: He's been feeding the goat treats to win it over, complicating the heist.

npcs.name: Jayne Cobb
npcs.motivation: Get paid and cause mayhem, but he has a soft spot for animals.
npcs.secret: He once had a pet goat as a child and is sentimentally attached.

npcs.name: Kaylee Frye
npcs.motivation: Keep Serenity running and find joy in the adventure.
npcs.secret: She's fascinated by the goat's possible mechanical enhancements and wants to study it.

npcs.name: Inara Serra
npcs.motivation: Maintain her reputation and subtly aid the crew.
npcs.secret: She's using the job to gather blackmail info on Lord Pompfrey for a client.

npcs.name: Simon Tam
npcs.motivation: Protect River and do no harm, despite the unethical job.
npcs.secret: He suspects the goat is a failed Alliance experiment and wants to expose it.

npcs.name: River Tam
npcs.motivation: Understand her abilities and enjoy the chaos.
npcs.secret: She can telepathically communicate with the goat, learning it just wants to be free.

npcs.name: Lord Pompfrey
npcs.motivation: Win a bet against his rival and gain social clout, no matter the cost.
npcs.secret: He plans to double-cross the crew and sell the goat to the Alliance for profit.

npcs.name: Butler Jenkins
npcs.motivation: Protect his master's estate and dignity at all costs.
npcs.secret: He's an undercover Alliance agent monitoring genetic experiments, and the goat is a target.

factions.name: The Alliance
factions.description: The bureaucratic governing body of the core planets, often meddling in outer planet affairs with covert ops.
factions.goals: Maintain control, suppress unauthorized genetic engineering, and clean up messes quietly.

factions.name: Persephone Nobility
factions.description: Wealthy, eccentric families engaged in petty rivalries and absurd wagers to pass the time.
factions.goals: Outdo each other in social standing, amusement, and collecting oddities.

world_rules: Travel between planets is slow via spaceships; no faster-than-light or teleportation.
The Alliance has authority in core worlds, but outer planets like Persephone are lawless and chaotic.
Technology is a mix of advanced and rustic; AI and robots are rare, favoring human ingenuity.

data.passage:
The air smells of expensive perfume and nervous sweat as you stride into Lord Pompfrey's extravagant Persephone estate. Crystal chandeliers cast glittering light across a sea of gaudy aristocrats, their chatter blending with the clink of champagne glasses. Zoe and Jayne flank you, looking about as comfortable as a pair of bandits at an Alliance gala.

The lord himself approaches, a portly man with a waxed mustache and a grin that screams 'trouble.' 'Captain Reynolds!' he booms, sloshing his drink. 'So glad you could make it to my little... gathering.'

Zoe raises an eyebrow. 'Little gathering? Place is packed tighter than a cargo hold during a smuggling run.'

Pompfrey chuckles. 'Indeed! Now, about that job I mentioned...' He leans in conspiratorially. 'There's a certain goat I need... acquired. Belongs to my rival, Lord Prendergast. Foolish wager, I know, but pride is at stake!'

A.text: "A goat?" you ask skeptically, crossing your arms. "You're paying us to steal livestock?"
A.consequence: Mal expresses skepticism, leading to more details about the job without negative repercussions.
A.narration_directive: Write Pompfrey elaborating on the goat and the wager, with humorous reactions from the crew.

B.text: "Let's cut to the chase," you say bluntly. "How much are we talking?"
B.consequence: Mal's bluntness may offend Pompfrey, potentially lowering the offered payment or reducing trust.
B.narration_directive: Describe Pompfrey's taken-aback reaction, then haggling over price, with Zoe and Jayne chiming in.

C.text: "A goat, huh?" you reply with a smirk. "Must be one hell of an animal."
C.consequence: Mal's smirk amuses Pompfrey, who reveals extra information about the goat's unusual traits.
C.narration_directive: Pompfrey laughs and shares anecdotes about the goat's antics, hinting at its genetic enhancements.

D.text: "You know what?" you say, turning to leave. "This sounds like a bad idea."
D.consequence: Mal refuses the job and leaves, ending the heist before it begins.
D.narration_directive: Write a comedic scene where Mal walks away, the crew shrugs, and the story concludes with a silly lesson about avoiding goat-related schemes.

data.passage:
Lord Pompfrey's monocle nearly pops out as you cut through his flowery speech. "Well! Such... directness," he huffs, adjusting his cravat. "Five thousand credits upon delivery of the beast."

Zoe raises an eyebrow. "That's a mighty specific sum for a livestock dispute. What aren't you telling us?"

Jayne cracks his knuckles. "I say we start at ten and let him talk us down to eight."

Pompfrey turns puce. "Preposterous! This is a simple retrieval job!" But you notice his eyes dart toward a portrait of his rival—a man smugly posing with the goat in question. The creature wears a tiny gold crown. River suddenly giggles. "The goat wears the crown but the man wears the leash," she murmurs.

The noble sighs dramatically. "Fine. Seven thousand. But only because I detest Lord Fancypants' smug face more than I dislike your manners."

A.text: Accept the deal with a handshake, then ask Inara to work her charm for estate details.
A.consequence: Deal accepted; Inara gathers estate details, improving planning efficiency.
A.narration_directive: Describe Inara gracefully extracting floor plans and guard schedules from Pompfrey or his staff.

B.text: Demand half upfront, citing 'goat-related uncertainties' with a straight face.
B.consequence: Pompfrey is offended, reduces payment to 5000 credits, and relationship decreases significantly.
B.narration_directive: Show Pompfrey's outrage, him slashing the offer, and Mal having to back down or face losing the job.

C.text: Have Kaylee inspect the goat's schematics—wait, schematics? Since when do goats have those?
C.consequence: Kaylee discovers the goat has mechanical enhancements, revealing goat_abilities_known flag early.
C.narration_directive: Kaylee excitedly points out fusion cells and servo joints in the schematics, hinting at the goat's capabilities.

D.text: Whisper to River: "What's so funny about that goat?" while Pompfrey isn't looking.
D.consequence: River reveals the goat is telepathic and bored, but her loud whisper draws Pompfrey's attention.
D.narration_directive: River explains the goat's thoughts, but Pompfrey overhears and becomes suspicious, questioning their intentions.

PACING — REQUIRED:
- The current act's target_scenes (in the skeleton) is the intended scene count for this act.
- Compute scenes elapsed in this act: scene_number - act_start_scene + 1 (both values are in CURRENT POSITION above).

TERMINAL OUTCOMES — CHOICE PRE-CLASSIFICATION:
- advances_act: Use when scenes_in_act >= MAX(3, CEIL(target_scenes * 0.5)) AND the choice would resolve the act's end_condition. At most ONE advances_act choice per set of four.
- conclusion: Only valid in Act 3. Use when the choice would resolve Act 3's end_condition and complete the story. At most ONE conclusion choice per set of four.
- game_over: Use when the choice leads to an irreversible failure appropriate to the tone (see STYLE & TONE above). At most ONE game_over choice per set of four.

- IMPORTANT: At most ONE terminal outcome (advances_act, conclusion, or game_over) per set of four choices. The other three choices must use normal outcomes.

TIME ELAPSED — REQUIRED EVERY TURN:
- You MUST include time_elapsed in state_updates to indicate how much in-game time passed during this scene.
- Estimate realistically: a brief conversation = 5-15 minutes, a fight = 1-5 minutes, traveling across a city = 30-60 minutes, a journey = hours or days, sleeping/resting = 6-8 hours.
- The client uses time_elapsed to advance the in-game clock.

HARD MODE RULES (MANDATORY):
- Consequences are severe by default. When the player makes a risky choice, the outcome MUST hurt — lost allies, destroyed resources, permanent relationship damage. Do NOT hedge or soften.
- Dangerous situations MUST feel dangerous: at least one choice per set SHOULD carry severe_penalty or worse when the narrative context warrants it.

PASSAGE:
Lord Pompfrey's monocle nearly pops out as you cut through his flowery speech. "Well! Such... directness," he huffs, adjusting his cravat. "Five thousand credits upon delivery of the beast."

Zoe raises an eyebrow. "That's a mighty specific sum for a livestock dispute. What aren't you telling us?"

Jayne cracks his knuckles. "I say we start at ten and let him talk us down to eight."

Pompfrey turns puce. "Preposterous! This is a simple retrieval job!" But you notice his eyes dart toward a portrait of his rival—a man smugly posing with the goat in question. The creature wears a tiny gold crown. River suddenly giggles. "The goat wears the crown but the man wears the leash," she murmurs.

The noble sighs dramatically. "Fine. Seven thousand. But only because I detest Lord Fancypants' smug face more than I dislike your manners."

D: Whisper to River: "What's so funny about that goat?" while Pompfrey isn't looking.

data.passage:
You clasp Lord Pompfrey's soft, bejeweled hand, sealing the deal with a firm shake that makes him wince slightly. Turning to Inara with a subtle nod, you watch as she rises with practiced elegance, her gown catching the candlelight. 'Lord Pompfrey,' she purrs, 'might I trouble you for a private word? Your estate is simply breathtaking, and I'd love to hear more about its... intricacies.'

Pompfrey, visibly dazzled, leads Inara toward his study with a spring in his step. The rest of the crew exchanges knowing looks - Jayne rolling his eyes, Zoe smirking, and River giggling into her tea. Minutes later, Inara returns with a sly smile, casually slipping a folded paper into your hand. You unfold it to find detailed floor plans and guard schedules, complete with notes in Inara's elegant script. 'He was most... accommodating,' she remarks, her tone dripping with amusement.

The crew gathers around, studying the plans. Zoe points out potential entry points while Kaylee marvels at the estate's elaborate security system. River quietly mutters something about 'goat telepathy,' but everyone wisely chooses to ignore her. The plan starts to take shape, though Jayne's insistence on 'just shooting our way in' is swiftly vetoed.

A.text: Request Simon's medical expertise to create a distraction involving a 'health emergency'.
A.consequence: Simon creates a distraction, but it draws minor suspicion from estate staff.

B.text: Ask Kaylee to disable the estate's security using her mechanical skills.
B.consequence: Kaylee successfully disables key security systems, making infiltration easier.
B.narration_directive: Show Kaylee tinkering with security panels, bypassing alarms with her mechanical genius.

C.text: Send River and Jayne to scout the perimeter for alternative entry points.
C.consequence: River and Jayne's scouting trip ends in a loud argument, alerting nearby guards and increasing security.
C.narration_directive: Portray River's erratic behavior and Jayne's frustration leading to a shouting match that draws guard attention.

D.text: Have Wash pose as a wealthy buyer to gain access to the estate's stables.
D.consequence: Wash gains access to the stables and learns the goat's location, but his cover is slightly compromised.
D.narration_directive: Describe Wash charming his way into the stables, discovering the goat's pen, but raising eyebrows with his nervous demeanor.

data.passage:
You nod to Wash, who straightens his vest and puts on his most charming grin. "Time to play the part of a wealthy goat enthusiast," he mutters, sauntering toward the estate's stables with exaggerated confidence. Zoe rolls her eyes but can't hide a smirk.

Inside, Wash charms the stable hands with tales of his "extensive goat collection" back on Londinium, though his nervous twitch when a goat bleats loudly nearly gives him away. He manages to locate the prized goat's pen—a lavish enclosure with a velvet rope barrier. The goat itself is... unusual. Its eyes gleam with an unsettling intelligence, and it watches Wash with what can only be described as amusement.

Just as Wash leans in for a closer look, a stern voice cuts through the stable. "And what, precisely, are you doing near Lord Harrington's champion?". Butler Jenkins, a wiry man with a disapproving frown, steps into view, his gaze sharp as a knife.

A.text: Have Inara intervene, using her Companion training to distract Jenkins with polite conversation.

B.text: Signal Kaylee to cause a diversion with a small, harmless explosion from her toolkit.

C.text: Whisper to River to use her psychic abilities to make Jenkins forget he saw anything.

D.text: Step forward yourself, pretending to be Wash's annoyed business partner there to rein him in.
